revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:жқұеӨ§д№…жүӢеҸӨеўі]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Higashi ЕҢkute Kofun | image = [[File:Higashi Okute Kofun, Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2019.jpg|250px|Higashi ЕҢkute Kofun (May 2019)]] | caption = Higashi ЕҢkute Kofun
(May 2019) {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | location = {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|qid=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|qid=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama Ward|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}}, Kamishidami, ЕҢaza ЕҢkute-shimo, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.251222|N|137.039817|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Mound length approx. 37.5m; total length (including moat) approx. 58m; rear circular section diameter approx. 26.5m; height unknown | built = Mid-{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} | burial = | Interred_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | alternate_name = | notes = }} '''Higashi ЕҢkute Kofun''' (жқұеӨ§д№…жүӢеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Kamishidami, Moriyama Ward, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|qid=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|qid=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Group|1=Shidami Kofun group}}. == Overview == The kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} situated in the area surrounding the ЕҢkute Pond, part of the ЕҢkute Kofun Group. It is also referred to as ЕҢkute Tomb No. 1. In {{ill|1982|en|1982|qid=Q2436|lt=1982|1=1982}}вҖ“{{ill|1983|en|1983|qid=Q2434|lt=1983|1=1983}} (Showa 57вҖ“58), a survey was conducted by Nagoya University at the request of the Aichi Prefecture Board of Education. Further excavations were carried out by the Nagoya City Board of Education beginning in {{ill|2005|en|2005|qid=Q2019|lt=2005|1=2005}} (Heisei 17). == area and Structure == Higashi ЕҢkute Kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a short rectangular front section. The mound is approximately 37.5 meters in length, with a total length of around 58 meters, including the surrounding moat. The rear circular section measures about 26.5 meters in diameter, though the exact height remains unknown due to significant flattening of the mound. Based on the preserved state of the rear circular section, the mound is believed to have been constructed in two tiers. Initially, Higashi ЕҢkute and {{ill|Nishi ЕҢkute Kofun|ja|иҘҝеӨ§д№…жүӢеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi ЕҢkute Kofun|qq=}} were thought to share the same axis and dimensions, indicating a close relationship. However, a 2005 excavation revealed discrepancies, including a shorter mound length and a misalignment of their axes. Additionally, the lack of angular changes in the front section's slope, as observed in Nishi ЕҢkute and {{ill|Shidami ЕҢtsuka Kofun|ja|еҝ—ж®өе‘іеӨ§еЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami ЕҢtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, suggests Higashi ЕҢkute Kofun may have stronger ties to local powers. burial goods such as {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} have been recovered. Stone arrangements, believed to be {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|qid=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}, and cylindrical haniwa were identified at the boundary between the front and rear sections. In {{ill|2008|en|2008|qid=Q2004|lt=2008|1=2008}} (Heisei 20), remnants of a circular haniwa row were discovered encircling the rear section. File:Otsuka and Okute kofun group.jpg|Higashi ЕҢkute Kofun (lower center-left) and Nishi ЕҢkute Kofun (lower left); Shidami ЕҢtsuka Kofun is located in the upper right (1987). {{National Land image Information}} File:Higashi Okute kofun02.jpg|Higashi ЕҢkute Kofun as of July 2008 == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the site are housed in the {{ill|Nagoya City Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|еҗҚеҸӨеұӢеёӮиҰӢжҷҙеҸ°иҖғеҸӨиіҮж–ҷйӨЁ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya City Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҰӢжҷҙеҸ°йҒәи·Ў|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11630197}}: * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} * {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} == References == * Aichi Prefecture Historical Compilation Committee (2005). ''Aichi Prefecture History, Volume 3: Archaeology - Kofun Period.'' Aichi Prefecture. * Nagoya City Board of Education (2006). ''Excavation Report on Shidami ЕҢtsuka Kofun and ЕҢkute Kofun Group - Fiscal Year 2005.'' * Editorial Committee for the New Nagoya City History (2008). ''New Nagoya City History, Volume 1: Archaeology.'' Nagoya City. == Access == * From {{ill|ЕҢzone Station|en|ЕҢzone Station|qid=Q872075|lt=ЕҢzone Station|1=ЕҢzone Station}} (JR ChЕ«ЕҚ Line, Meitetsu Seto Line, Nagoya Municipal Subway MeijЕҚ Line) * From {{ill|KЕҚzЕҚji Station|en|KЕҚzЕҚji Station|qid=Q5359256|lt=KЕҚzЕҚji Station|1=KЕҚzЕҚji Station}} (JR ChЕ«ЕҚ Line, Aichi Loop Railway): ''' Take the {{ill|Nagoya Guideway Bus Shidami Line|ja|еҗҚеҸӨеұӢгӮ¬гӮӨгғүгӮҰгӮ§гӮӨгғҗгӮ№гӮ¬гӮӨгғүгӮҰгӮ§гӮӨгғҗгӮ№еҝ—ж®өе‘із·ҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Guideway Bus Shidami Line|1=Guideway Bus Shidami Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q850582}} (Yutorito Line) and alight at the "Higashitani Bridge" bus stop. == See Also == * {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|qid=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Group|1=Shidami Kofun group}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®еҸӨеўідёҖиҰ§|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kurashi/kyoiku/bunka/rekishinosato/nagoya00057522.html Higashi ЕҢkute Kofun (Nagoya City official site)] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|жқұеӨ§д№…жүӢеҸӨеўі|version=92913982|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:жқұеӨ§д№…жүӢеҸӨеўі]] {{Commons category|Higashi Okute Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11526448}} [[Category:History of Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagoya]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Shidami Kofun group]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
